title: Enforcing the Farming Rules for Water
description: Criteria for the Environment Agency to use when enforcing the Farming Rules for Water, particularly for autumn and winter applications of organic manures.

title: Bird flu: licence to move and spread used poultry litter, manure and slurry in a protection or surveillance zone (EXD353(HPAI)(EW))
description: General licence to move from premises and spread poultry litter, manure and slurry in a protection or surveillance zone in England and Wales.

title: Code of Good Agricultural Practice for reducing ammonia emissions
description: This Code of Good Agricultural Practice (COGAP) provides best practice guidance for reducing ammonia emissions from farms in England.

title: Farming Equipment and Technology Fund (FETF) 2024 (closed)
description: A closed FETF grant for help to buy items that improve productivity, manage slurry and improve animal health and welfare.

title: Farming Equipment and Technology Fund (FETF) 2025 (closed)
description: A closed FETF grant for help to buy items that improve productivity, manage slurry and improve animal health and welfare.

title: Farming Equipment and Technology Fund (FETF) 2026 (Closed)
description: A grant to help buy items that improve productivity, manage slurry and improve animal health and welfare. Applications closed at midday on 12 May 2026

title: Intensive farming: comply with your environmental permit
description: How to comply with the conditions in your environmental permit for intensive pig and poultry farming.

title: Landspreading: apply to deploy mobile plant
description: Information you must provide in your application to deploy mobile plant for landspreading waste. How you must operate under your approved deployment.

title: Landspreading: how to comply with your permit
description: How to meet the rules in your permit if you use mobile plant to spread waste to land for agricultural benefit or ecological improvement.

title: Low risk waste positions: effluent and sludge
description: The Environment Agency has provided these low risk waste positions (LRWPs) for waste operations that it considers may be suitable for an exemption.

title: Manure, fertilisers and soil improvers - Import Information Notes
description: Guidance for vets, importers and border control officials when importing manure, fertilisers and soil improvers to Great Britain.

title: Pig and Poultry Assurance Scheme
description: Guidance about the Pig and Poultry Assurance Scheme available under the Environmental Permitting (England and Wales) Regulations 2010.

title: Slurry Infrastructure grant round 1: guidance for invited applicants
description: Invited applicants can apply for a grant to improve or expand slurry storage capacity to 6 months.

title: Slurry Infrastructure grant: information for local planning authorities
description: Advice from Natural England on planning applications for slurry management on farms.

title: Slurry Infrastructure grant round 2: guidance for invited applicants
description: Invited applicants can apply for a grant to improve or expand slurry storage capacity and to cover stores.

title: Solidifying wet utilities excavation waste using non-waste absorbents: RPS 275
description: Environment Agency regulatory position on using non-waste absorbents to solidify saturated soils and slurry wastes produced during construction and excavation works.

title: SR2010 No 17: storage of wastes to be used in land treatment
description: Standard rules to allow storage of wastes to be used in land treatment.

title: SR2010 No 4: mobile plant for land-spreading
description: Standard rules to operate mobile plant for land spreading and deployment form.

title: SR2010 No 6: mobile plant for landspreading of sewage sludge
description: Standard rules to operate mobile plant for landspreading of sewage sludge and deployment form.

title: SR2021 No 10: anaerobic digestion of non-hazardous sludge at a waste water treatment works, including the use of the resultant biogas
description: For a Part A installation with a biological treatment capacity exceeding 100 tonnes each day, accepting no more than 500,000 tonnes a year.

title: SR2021 No 8: on-farm anaerobic digestion facility using farm wastes only, including use of the resultant biogas – installations
description: For a Part A installation with an anaerobic digestion capacity of over 100 tonnes of waste, or a combination of waste and non-waste each day and accepting no more than 100,000 tonnes per year.

title: SR2021 No 9: on-farm anaerobic digestion facility, including use of the resultant biogas – waste recovery operation
description: For a facility with a treatment capacity of less than 100 tonnes of waste, or a combination of waste and non-waste each day accepting no more than 35,000 tonnes each year.

title: SR2023 No 1: capture, treatment and storage of biogas from lagoons and tanks
description: Standard rules to capture, treat and store biogas from lagoons and tanks.

title: Storing and using manure to breed worms for a wormery: RPS 316
description: Environment Agency regulatory position on storing and using farmyard and horse manure to breed worms to make a wormery (vermiculture).

title: Storing and treating litter, straw, slurry and washwater from an animal disease outbreak: RPS 36
description: Environment Agency regulatory position on storing and treating litter, straw, slurry and washwater in a location where an animal disease outbreak has been identified by the Animal and Plant Health Agency.

title: Use of clay in slurry lagoons or irrigation reservoirs: RPS 91
description: Environment Agency regulatory position on using waste clay in the construction of slurry lagoons or irrigation/winter storage reservoirs.

title: Using waste codes for sludge materials: RPS 231
description: Environment Agency regulatory position on the use of additional waste codes to store, treat or spread sewage sludge and septic tank sludge to land.

title: Ammonia emission factors for pig and poultry screening, modelling and reporting
description: You can use these emission factors to quantify the amount of ammonia emitted from your permitted pig or poultry operations.

title: Construct a new or substantially enlarge an existing silage, slurry or agricultural fuel oil store, or store field silage on a new site
description: Use this service to notify the Environment Agency if you plan to construct a new or substantially enlarge an existing silage, slurry or agricultural fuel oil store, or store field silage on a new site.

title: Burn poultry manure in a combustion unit
description: How to get approval to burn poultry manure in a combustion unit on your farm or holding.

title: Grassland derogations for livestock manure in nitrate vulnerable zones
description: What to do if the livestock manure produced on your farm is likely to exceed the amount you're allowed to spread on your land in an NVZ (farm limit).

title: Handling of manure and slurry to reduce antibiotic resistance
description: Guidance on the handling of slurry and manure to help reduce the spread of antibiotic resistant bacteria.

title: How to do the SFI actions for nutrient management
description: Find out how you could do the SFI actions for nutrient management.

title: How to use, store or move manure, guano and digestive tract content
description: Find out how you can use manure, guano and digestive tract content, including how to store and move them.

title: Intensive pig and poultry farm inspections
description: Information for farmers about inspections the Environment Agency carries out on pig and poultry farm structures, records and, maintenance and management plans.

title: Landspreading: benefits and risks of the waste types you can use
description: Information on waste codes and descriptions to use when landspreading to improve soil health.

title: Landspreading to improve soil health
description: How to meet the requirements of using mobile plant for landspreading waste to provide agricultural benefit or ecological improvement.

title: Pigs and poultry intensive farming: environmental permits
description: How to apply for an environmental permit if you rear pigs and poultry intensively, including how to comply with and change the permit.

title: Preventing agricultural pollution in exceptional circumstances
description: Spreading slurry and milk on agricultural land: Environment Agency advice in exceptional circumstances such as extreme weather.

title: Processing manure for sale as fertiliser in the EU
description: How to process manure, guano or digestive tract content for sale as a fertiliser in the EU, including how to propose a new treatment method.

title: Report nitrogen and phosphorus emissions from intensive pig and poultry farms
description: Calculate the annual nitrogen and phosphorus emissions from your livestock. Report the data to the Environment Agency.

title: Request an ammonia screening assessment
description: Use these forms to request an ammonia screening assessment as part of an intensive farming permit application.

title: Rules for farmers and land managers
description: What you must do when you keep livestock or manage land.

title: How to comply with the Farming Rules for Water
description: What you must do to prevent water pollution when you manage organic manures, manufactured fertiliser, soil and livestock.

title: Storing organic manures in nitrate vulnerable zones
description: How to provide enough storage and keep storage records of organic manures in a nitrate vulnerable zone (NVZ).

title: Storing silage, slurry and agricultural fuel oil
description: Rules you must follow, who’s responsible, when to notify the Environment Agency and what to do if you get an enforcement notice.

title: Using a mechanical slurry separator to manage your slurry
description: What farmers and land managers must know about slurry separators, storing and applying separated materials.

title: Temporary storage where waste is produced (NWFD 2 exemption)
description: You can temporarily store any waste at the place of production before it’s collected. You do not need to register this exemption.

title: S3: storing sludge
description: S3 waste exemption lets you store sewage sludge at a site where it will be used in accordance with the Sludge (Use in Agriculture) Regulations 1989.

title: T24: anaerobic digestion on farms
description: T24 exemption allows farmers to anaerobically digest manure, slurry and vegetation on their farms to produce digestate for use as fertiliser or soil conditioner.

title: U10: spreading waste to benefit agricultural land
description: U10 exemption lets you spread certain wastes on agricultural land to improve soil, instead of using manufactured fertilisers or virgin materials.

title: U11: spreading waste on non-agricultural land
description: U11 exemption lets you spread waste on non-agricultural land to improve soil, instead of using manufactured fertilisers or virgin materials.

title: U15: mixing pig and poultry ash with manure
description: U15 exemption allows you to mix ash from burning pig or poultry with slurry or manure, and spread it on farmland to provide the soil with nutrients.
